heading: Seizure; judicial forfeiture proceedings
label: 1610 Seizure; judicial forfeiture proceedings
text: If any vessel, vehicle, aircraft, merchandise, or baggage is not subject to , the appropriate customs officer shall transmit a report of the case, with the names of available witnesses, to the United States attorney for the district in which the seizure was made for the institution of the proper proceedings for the condemnation of such property.section 1607 of this title
notes: Prior ProvisionsProvisions similar to those in this section were contained in , . That section was superseded by section 610 of act , comprising this section, and repealed by section 651(a)(1) of the 1930 act.act Sept. 21, 1922, ch. 356, title IV, § 61042 Stat. 985June 17, 1930

Amendments1988— reenacted section without change.Pub. L. 100–6901984— substituted “If any vessel, vehicle, aircraft, merchandise, or baggage is not subject to ” for “If the value of any vessel, vehicle, merchandise, or baggage so seized is greater than $10,000”.Pub. L. 98–573section 1607 of this titleamended section in manner substantially identical to amendment by .Pub. L. 98–473Pub. L. 98–5731978— substituted “$10,000” for “$2,500” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 95–4101970— substituted reference to appropriate customs officer for reference to collector, and struck out reference to appraiser’s return of value.Pub. L. 91–2711954—Act , substituted “$2,500” for “$1,000”.Sept. 1, 1954
Effective Date of 1984 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 98–573section 214(e) of Pub. L. 98–573section 1304 of this titleOct. 15, 1984
